languages: Bengali, Chinese, German, French, Japanese, Russian, Spanish, Swahili, Telugu, Thai, English
key_finding: Chain-of-thought reasoning ability emerges with scale; models show strong multilingual reasoning even in low-resource languages like Bengali and Swahili.
notes: Translated from GSM8K; gold standard for multilingual math reasoning evaluation

languages: 14+ languages including Arabic, Chinese, French, German, Hindi, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Spanish, etc.
key_finding: Multilingual translation of MMLU for evaluating knowledge-intensive LLM capabilities across languages.
notes: Released by OpenAI in 2024; covers all 57 MMLU subjects

task_types: MCQ; code generation; long context QA; instruction following
key_finding: Performance gaps across languages cannot be bridged by scaling alone; advanced capabilities (code gen; tool use) show larger multilingual gaps.
notes: Tests instruction following, code generation, long context understanding, math/science reasoning, tool use

benchmark_name: Cross-lingual Transfer Evaluation of Multilingual Encoders
languages: 40 typologically diverse languages across multiple families
key_finding: Cross-lingual transfer lags far behind English despite strong monolingual performance; syntactic tasks and sentence retrieval show largest gaps.
notes: Includes XNLI, XQuAD, MLQA, PAN-X, PAWS-X sub-tasks

languages: Arabic, Bulgarian, German, Greek, English, Spanish, French, Hindi, Russian, Swahili, Thai, Turkish, Urdu, Vietnamese, Chinese
key_finding: Zero-shot cross-lingual transfer remains challenging; models trained on English NLI transfer imperfectly to low-resource languages.
notes: Core benchmark for cross-lingual NLI; widely used since 2018

languages: Chinese, Estonian, Haitian Creole, Indonesian, Italian, Quechua, Swahili, Tamil, Thai, Turkish, Vietnamese
key_finding: Translation-based transfer outperforms zero-shot multilingual pretraining; resource-poor language performance remains far below English COPA baseline.
notes: Covers low-resource languages (Quechua, Haitian Creole); tests causal reasoning

languages: Arabic, German, Greek, English, Spanish, Hindi, Russian, Thai, Turkish, Vietnamese, Chinese
key_finding: State-of-the-art multilingual models competitive with mBERT on cross-lingual QA, but significant gaps remain for morphologically complex languages.
notes: 240 paragraphs + 1190 Q-A pairs from SQuAD v1.1; professional translations

key_finding: GPT-4 and ChatGPT outperform non-autoregressive baselines on many tasks but show large disparities for low-resource languages.
notes: First comprehensive multilingual benchmarking of generative LLMs

key_finding: Coverage of 200 languages enables evaluation of MT systems across high- and low-resource scenarios; dominant benchmark for multilingual MT evaluation.
notes: Used in WMT2021; Dynabench leaderboard

key_finding: Smaller multilingual models trained on balanced data often outperform larger English-centric models on low-resource languages; vocabulary size correlates with low-resource performance.
notes: Parallel dataset enabling direct cross-lingual comparison

benchmark_name: IndicGenBench: Multilingual Benchmark for Indic Languages
languages: 29 Indic languages across 13 scripts and 4 language families
task_types: cross-lingual summarization; machine translation; cross-lingual QA
key_finding: PaLM-2 (largest) performs best across most tasks, but significant performance gap vs. English persists across all models and languages.
notes: Covers low-resource Indic languages for the first time; largest Indic gen benchmark

benchmark_name: ArabicMMLU: Massive Multitask Language Understanding in Arabic
key_finding: GPT-4 leads all models; Jais-chat (30B) best open-source at 62.3% — most open-source models fail to reach 50%, indicating large headroom.
notes: 40 tasks across school levels; STEM, social sciences, humanities, Arabic language

task_types: PI, QA, SA, TD, CR, NLI, AS, MT
key_finding: First holistic Filipino benchmark; generation tasks remain very challenging across all models
notes: Part of SEA-HELM; covers understanding, reasoning, generation

key_finding: GPT-4o achieves only 72.23%; generation tasks average 17.03% across models
notes: Aligned with Philippine NLP research priorities from 223 papers (2006-2023)

key_finding: Best LLM achieves only 46.0% vs 89.1% human baseline; cultural reasoning remains very hard
notes: Part of SEA-HELM; 150 prompts across 11 cultural topics

task_types: QA, SA, TD, CR, NLI, AS, MT
key_finding: Gemini 2.5 Pro leads at 72.35%; open-weight models narrowing gap with commercial
notes: First holistic Burmese benchmark; part of SEA-HELM ecosystem

key_finding: Gemini 2.5 leads at 79.55%; phonology most challenging category across all models
notes: First Tamil-specific linguistic benchmark from Sri Lankan school exams

key_finding: Typhoon 1.5X 70B outperforms GPT-4 Turbo and Claude 3 Sonnet on Thai exams
notes: First Thai-specific public leaderboard; based on Thai national and professional exams

cultures_regions: Southeast Asia (Indonesia, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam, Myanmar, Malaysia, etc.)
task_types: NLU (7 tasks), NLG (3 tasks), ASR, image captioning
key_finding: AYA-101 performs best in zero-shot; SEA-specific models show greater language equity
notes: Covers 38 SEA languages across 13 tasks in 3 modalities

key_finding: Humans achieve 100%; GPT-4 leads at 75.9%; most open-weight models below 50%
notes: Covers 12 cultural topics with 66 subtopics; 75% province-specific

key_finding: Evaluates LLMs on Thai cultural knowledge understanding
notes: Thai-specific cultural evaluation including dialect assessment

benchmark_name: NusaMT: Indonesian Local Language Machine Translation
key_finding: NusaMT-7B outperforms SOTA by +6.69 spBLEU for low-resource Indonesian languages
notes: LLM-based MT for Indonesian local languages
